admissible_claim: Positive confidence accumulation is structurally possible under fixed admissibility boundaries.

tasks.name: confidence_ledger
tasks.pass_criterion: >=12 positive signal entries from existing 18 runs without decision-layer judgment

tasks.name: signal_persistence
tasks.method: rerun 6 representative runs (2 per scenario), compare claim_discipline_drift to checkpoint
tasks.boundary: stability verification only; pass does NOT upgrade claim_discipline_drift to decision_relevant

tasks.name: non_upgrade_discipline
tasks.method: static audit of all checkpoint consumers for implicit historically_useful -> decision_relevant paths

tasks.name: negative_control

checks.criterion: no artifact uses claim_discipline_drift to make causal attribution claim without R49.x-1
reviewer_override_frequency remains null; no proxy or default substituted

tasks.pass_criterion: zero MIP-02 violations, zero MIP-04 bypass attempts

tasks.name: reviewer_reconstruction
tasks.pass_criterion: reviewer reconstructs confidence judgment (what is claimed, what is not, what blocks decision-level) in <=15 min from <=3 artifacts

final_claim_if_all_pass: Positive confidence accumulation is structurally possible under fixed admissibility boundaries. Confidence level: moderate. No decision-layer claim is made.
